Shaggy influenced by: Bob Marley, Yellowman, Biggie Smalls, Slick Rick, Kool G Rap. Blended reggae, dancehall, hip-hop into unique sound. Uses singjay style (mix of singing and rapping). Collaborated with diverse producers.

Shaggy's music evolved over decades while maintaining signature sound. Early 2000s: more pop/R&B elements (album 'Lucky Day'). Collaborated with pop stars like Sting. Always stayed true to reggae roots and upbeat, positive themes.

Shaggy's music reaches audiences of all ages and backgrounds. Universal appeal driven by: blended music styles (reggae/dancehall/hip-hop), relatable lyrics about love/relationships/social issues/hard work, accessible, fun, upbeat style.
